name: enforce-file natively selects a scraped terraform iac-dialects rule on a tf file under include-app-code and skips it without the flag so cloud IaC rules are enforced when the routed tool is absent
command:
PID=cl564e01$$; G="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards"; O=/tmp/cl564e01-$$; mkdir -p $O $G/_project/$PID/us-government; printf -- '---
source: {id: t}
rules:
- id: g-us-government-t-tf-encrypt
  name: tf-encrypt
  description: "Terraform aws_s3_bucket MUST set server_side_encryption_configuration."
  detector: cloud-guidance-rule.sh
  severity: P1
  applies_to: {linguist_aliases: [hcl], iac_dialects: [terraform]}
' > $G/_project/$PID/us-government/t.yaml; printf 'resource "aws_s3_bucket" "b" {}
' > $O/main.tf; BASE=$(bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/enforce-file.sh" --file $O/main.tf --include-app-code 2>&1 | grep -oE 'rules_checked=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2); WITH=$(bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/enforce-file.sh" --file $O/main.tf --project $PID --include-app-code 2>&1 | grep -oE 'rules_checked=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2); GATED=$(bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/enforce-file.sh" --file $O/main.tf --project $PID 2>&1 | grep -oE 'rules_checked=[0-9]+' | cut -d= -f2); rm -rf "$G/_project/$PID" $O; [ -n "$BASE" ] && [ "$WITH" -eq $((BASE+1)) ] && [ "$GATED" -eq "$BASE" ] && echo iac-rule-natively-selectable 1>&2
